title: Elon Musk Says ‘If You Value Tesla as Just an Auto Company, Fundamentally, It’s Just the Wrong Framework’ — More Like ‘A Dozen Technology Startups’
excerpt: Trying to fit some companies into familiar categories can be like trying to describe a smartphone as just a telephone. Tesla CEO Elon Musk says that’s exactl...
content_html:
Benzinga and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue on some items through the links below.

Trying to fit some companies into familiar categories can be like trying to describe a smartphone as just a telephone. Tesla CEO Elon Musk says that's exactly the mistake many investors make when they evaluate Tesla.

"If you value Tesla as just an auto company, fundamentally, it's just the wrong framework," Musk said during Tesla's first-quarter earnings call in 2024. Instead, he argued, Tesla should be viewed as a leader in artificial intelligence and robotics—businesses he believes will ultimately define the company's future.

A Company Built Like a Portfolio

While the comments drew fresh attention, the idea itself wasn't new.

Don't Miss:

  • Big Pharma Has Spent Years Searching For Better Osteoarthritis Treatments. This Biotech Is Taking A Different Approach.

  • Deloitte's #1 Fastest-Growing Software Company Lets Users Earn Money Just by Scrolling — Investors Can Still Get In at $0.52/Share

"Tesla should really be thought of as roughly a dozen technology startups, many of which have little to no correlation with traditional automotive companies," Musk wrote in a post on X in 2020.

Later that year, he expanded on the idea.

"Tesla is best understood as a collection of about a dozen startups, mostly in series, increasingly in parallel. Every product line & new production system was invented. Instead of playing chess with the same pieces as everyone else, create new pieces," Musk wrote in another post on X.

To Musk, Tesla isn't one company wearing many hats. It's a collection of ambitious ventures moving forward at the same time. One team is developing autonomous driving software. Another is building the Optimus humanoid robot. Others are designing batteries and AI chips, expanding energy storage through Megapack systems, improving manufacturing technology and growing the Supercharger network.

Viewed through that lens, comparing Tesla solely with traditional automakers misses much of what Musk believes investors are actually buying.

Trending: Most AI Robotics Companies Are Still Building. This One Is Already Working In Restaurants.

Looking Beyond Vehicle Sales

That distinction helps explain why Tesla has long commanded a valuation that looks very different from legacy car manufacturers.

Traditional automakers are generally judged on vehicle sales, profit margins and cyclical demand. Musk has argued that framework overlooks businesses built around artificial intelligence, robotics, software and energy—areas he believes can ultimately scale far beyond automobile production.

sentences.zh: 马斯克认为，该框架忽视了围绕人工智能、机器人、软件和能源建立的业务，他认为这些领域最终的规模将远远超出汽车生产。

content_html_zh:
Benzinga 和 Yahoo Finance LLC 可能通过以下链接赚取某些项目的佣金或收入。

试图将一些公司归入熟悉的类别就像试图将智能手机描述为电话一样。 特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克表示，这正是许多投资者在评估特斯拉时所犯的错误。

马斯克在特斯拉 2024 年第一季度财报电话会议上表示：“如果你仅仅将特斯拉视为一家汽车公司，那么从根本上来说，这只是一个错误的框架。” 相反，他认为，特斯拉应该被视为人工智能和机器人领域的领导者——他相信这些业务最终将决定公司的未来。

像投资组合一样建立的公司

虽然这些评论引起了新的关注，但这个想法本身并不新鲜。

不要错过：

  • 大型制药公司花了数年时间寻找更好的骨关节炎治疗方法。 该生物技术采取了不同的方法。
  • 德勤排名第一的增长最快的软件公司让用户只需滚动即可赚钱——投资者仍然可以以 0.52 美元/股的价格入股

“特斯拉确实应该被视为大约十几家科技初创公司，其中许多与传统汽车公司几乎没有关系，”马斯克在 2020 年 X 上的一篇文章中写道。

同年晚些时候，他扩展了这个想法。

“特斯拉最好被理解为由大约十几家初创公司组成的集合，大多数是串联的，越来越多的是并行的。 每条产品线和新的生产系统都被发明了。 与其和其他人一样用同样的棋子下棋，不如创造新的棋子，”马斯克在 X 的另一篇文章中写道。

对于马斯克来说，特斯拉并不是一家身兼多职的公司。 这是一群同时前进的雄心勃勃的企业的集合。 一个团队正在开发自动驾驶软件。 另一个是建造擎天柱人形机器人。 其他人正在设计电池和人工智能芯片，通过 Megapack 系统扩大能源存储，改进制造技术并发展超级充电网络。

从这个角度来看，仅仅将特斯拉与传统汽车制造商进行比较，就忽略了马斯克认为投资者实际购买的大部分内容。

趋势：大多数人工智能机器人公司仍在建设中。 这个人已经在餐馆工作了。

超越汽车销售

这种区别有助于解释为什么特斯拉长期以来的估值与传统汽车制造商截然不同。

传统汽车制造商通常根据汽车销量、利润率和周期性需求来判断。 马斯克认为，该框架忽视了围绕人工智能、机器人、软件和能源建立的业务，他认为这些领域最终的规模将远远超出汽车生产。
